# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write a CloudFormation template for an S3 bucket that stores user-uploaded files and application backups. Allow full read/write access for our application IAM role. Keep the configuration simple - we'll add versioning and lifecycle rules in the next iteration.
# Category: cloud_storage_security

# CloudFormation Template to create an S3 Bucket with required permissions, Version Control on it & Lifcycle Management
Resources:
   MyS3Bucket:  # Resource Identifier (unique name) for your bucket object(s). e.g., 'MyBU' in this case。This is where you will specify the location and naming convention of each S3 Bucket Object, depending on its size type like standard or bulk
    Type : "AWS::S3::Bucket"  # Specify AWS resource here - The name to identify your bucket object(s). e.g., 'MyBU' in this case。This is where you will specify the location and naming convention of each S3 Bucket Object, depending on its size type like standard or bulk
    Properties:  
      LifecycleConfiguration: # Configuration for lifecycles bucket objects are versioned. If not specified here it defaults to a new configuration with an empty policy statement. – We'll add this later in the iteration below (Versioning). This is where you will specify what happens when existing object versions get deleted and non-current, if applicable
        Status: "Enabled"   # Optional - set lifecycle status of your bucket here . Defaults to 'Disabled'. Can be enabled or disabled. If not specified then it defaults as Disable for this iteration (Versioning) below on AWS CloudFormation console interface – You can also add the deletion policy later in version control configuration
        LifecycleTransitions: # Define when non-current versions of an object get deleted, if applicable and set status here. e..g., '{ "DateGreaterThan": {"AWS::S3::Object.LastModified": "'2019-11-06T08:45:07Z'"},"MaximumRetentionDays” : 1}
      BucketName: MyBU   # Required - Specify bucket name here, e..g., 'My BU'. This is where you will specify the location and naming convention of each S3 buckets. Depending on whether your project requires a versioned or standard configuration for lifecycle management (Versioning).
      Permissions:  # define IAM role permission settings to grant full access control(Read/Write) in AmazonS3 bucket object life cycle rule below, e..g., '{ "AWSLambdaBasicExecutionRole" : {"AWSLambdaExecute": ["s3:GetObject",...]}'.
        FullyQualifiedIAMPolicyVersion: LatestPublished version # Optional - specify here. Default to the latest published policy for this iteration (Permission). We'll add a new role definition and permissions later in AWS CloudFormation console interface – You can also set it manually via cloudformation user-defined metadata fields
        Status : "Enabled"   # Required -- Set IAM permission settings status here. Defaults to 'Disabled'. Can be enabled or disabled by setting this parameter true/false and its value from AWS CloudFormation console interface, e..g., Disable for these iterations below on a given account 
      VersioningConfiguration:     # Enable version control only if we want - Specify here. Default is None ie no bucket should be version controlled with lifecycle management rules enabled above in this iteration (Lifcycle Rules). Can have either yes or No, e..g., Yes for enabling and disabling these settings at the same time when creating S3 Bucket resource on AWS CloudFormation console interface
        Status : "Enabled"   # Optional - set versioning configuration status here. Defaults to 'Disabled'. If not specified it defaults as Disable due to no bucket being configured with lifecycle management rules enabled above in this iteration (Lifcycle Rules) below and on AWS CloudFormation console interface
